reviewer.evidencePolicy: Only mark the binary-label, possible-evidence preflight, and review-result evidence-upgrade boundaries satisfied; do not infer semantic approval of any possible evidence ref.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names portable label validation and the boundary between binary labels and richer reviewer-owned grouping.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by runtime and adapter tests covering portable user/developer/unclear audience labels, rejection of unsupported audience hints, adapter-owned semantic groups, and fallback semantic grouping.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep adapter validation and review clustering tests aligned when changing audience labels or semantic grouping behavior.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic possible-evidence preflight and final interpretation ownership.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by review-input tests that possible evidence refs can be attached as preflight hints while evidenceStatus remains unknown until review-result semantics verify the evidence.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep possible-evidence preflight output explicitly non-satisfying unless a verified review result upgrades the claim.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names evidence-status upgrade rules enforced by review-result application and claim validation.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by review-result and validation tests requiring direct or verified evidence refs before `evidenceStatus=satisfied`, while allowing review-result packets to update evidence state when semantics are met.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep claim-review result validation strict when changing evidence status upgrade semantics.
